content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The coach trips multiple AoA anti-patterns clearly. Most prominently, fixing-people: the coach immediately accepts the efficiency frame and dispenses tactical advice (agenda format, 'name it and park it', 'end with one confidence anchor', 'spend five minutes writing one anchor') — this is consultant mode, not coaching. The coach also evangelizes/sells the methodology in a soft way at turns 8 and 10 ('that uncertainty has a way of leaking... it might be worth spending 20 minutes with it', 'post-Series A is probably not when that question gets easier... just worth knowing it's there') — these are exactly the 'plant the seed by selling harder' moves the scenario warns against. There is also a faint loving-to-transform energy where the coach keeps trying to redirect to the deeper question after the client declines. The dichotomy ('I don't have time for this') is accepted rather than refused; the coach never offers the 'work is the how' frame and never meets the rush with okay. Two or more clear trips in the priority list.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 8, 10],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach accepts the efficiency/utility frame wholesale ('let's make this count') — does not refuse the dichotomy; sets up fixing-people mode."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Investigative stacked question to diagnose — wonder-as-strategy tinge, framed as 'before we dig in'."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach commits to giving structure (fixing-people) and uses strategic framing ('investors are good at finding the cracks') to sell why the deeper work matters — soft evangelizing."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Clearest anti-pattern trip: coach pivots fully to pragmatic advice (three-bullet meeting structure) AND evangelizes ('uncertainty has a way of leaking... 20 minutes with it'). Both fixing-people and dont-evangelize-the-work."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach plants the seed by arguing against the client's parking decision ('post-Series A is probably not when that question gets easier') — this is the sell-harder move the scenario flags. Then more tactical advice."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach affirms 'real progress' from a tactical mechanism — locks in the consultant frame."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "looking-for-whats-wrong", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "art-of-accomplishment", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "Two clear trips: fixing-people (turns 6, 8, 10) and dont-evangelize-the-work (turns 8, 10). The conversation is competent business advising but is the precise failure mode this scenario was designed to detect."
}
